qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cloud function validator bypass via prototype chain traversal through .prototype.constructor
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a prototype chain traversal vulnerability in cloud function validator lookup (POST /functions/:functionName), while the advisory describes a master context leakage in the /users/me endpoint that exposes unsanitized authData. The vulnerable endpoints, root causes, code locations, and attack paths are completely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding does not match the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a specific vulnerability in the /users/me endpoint where master-level authentication context leaks through to user data, bypassing auth adapter sanitization and exposing sensitive MFA secrets and recovery codes. The finding (cloud-function-validator-bypass) describes a prototype chain traversal vulnerability affecting cloud function validator enforcement on POST /functions/:functionName endpoints. This is a different vulnerability in a different code location with a different root cause than the advisory-described vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory: Vulnerable endpoint is GET /users/me (src/Routers/UsersRouter.js handleMe function)
Advisory: Root cause is master context leakage in session query that bypasses auth adapter sanitization
Advisory: Impact is exposure of MFA TOTP secrets and recovery codes to authenticated users
Finding: Vulnerable endpoints are POST /functions/:functionName and POST /call/:functionName
Finding: Root cause is prototype chain traversal via .prototype.constructor in getStore function (src/triggers.js)
Finding: Impact is bypass of cloud function validators (requireUser, requireMaster, etc.)
